articles.title: Hackers Exploit Gravity SMTP WordPress Plugin Bug to Expose API Keys
articles.summary: Threat actors are exploiting a recently patched security flaw impacting Gravity SMTP, a WordPress plugin that's installed on about 100,000 sites. articles.title: Klue OAuth breach victim list grows as Icarus hackers claim attack
articles.summary: Market intelligence platform Klue has publicly confirmed a recent security incident that allowed threat actors to steal OAuth tokens used to connect to customers' Salesforce environments, as the new "Icarus" extortion group publicly claims the attack. [...]

articles.title: Hackers exploit info disclosure bug in Gravity SMTP WordPress plugin
articles.summary: Threat actors are exploiting an unauthenticated information disclosure vulnerability in the WordPress plugin Gravity SMTP, active on 100,000 sites. [...]

articles.title: Unpatchable 'usbliter8' Exploit Breaks Apple A12 and A13 SecureROM Boot Chain
articles.summary: Security researchers at Paradigm Shift have published a working exploit, dubbed usbliter8, that achieves arbitrary code execution inside the SecureROM of Apple's A12 and A13 chips. That code is burned into the silicon at manufacture. No software update can reach it. Affected devices w

articles.title: The Gentlemen RaaS Uses GentleKiller EDR Framework Targeting 400 Security Processes
articles.summary: The Gentlemen ransomware-as-a-service (RaaS) operation is actively developing and maintaining a suite of endpoint detection and response (EDR) killers that it hands out to affiliates for impairing system defenses before deploying the encryptor. articles.title: Texas govt data breach exposes over 3 million driver’s licenses
articles.summary: The Texas Parks and Wildlife Department (TPWD) disclosed a data breach at its license system vendor that exposed personal information for more than three million individuals. [...]

articles.title: AutoJack Attack Lets One Web Page Hijack AI Agent for Host Code Execution
articles.summary: Microsoft researchers have detailed an exploit chain, named AutoJack, that turns an AI browsing agent into a delivery vehicle for remote code execution. Steer the agent to load an attacker's web page, and that page's JavaScript can reach a privileged local service on the same machine and spawn

articles.summary: Other noteworthy stories that might have slipped under the radar: Android TV botnet Popa linked to Israeli firm, Velvet Ant maintained decade-long stealth, unpatched GCP Config Connector flaw enables takeover. articles.summary: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Thursday urged Fortinet customers with FortiGate appliances to take steps to secure against ongoing malicious activity aimed at thousands of internet-accessible devices. articles.title: Police raid malware network tied to Russia's Evil Corp hacker group
articles.summary: An international operation targeted the SocGholish botnet, which has been linked to the Russia-based cybercrime group Evil Corp.

articles.title: Webinar: How attackers bypass MFA and how defenders can respond
articles.summary: Modern phishing attacks, including Device Code phishing, can undermine MFA protections and grant attackers access to corporate accounts without stealing passwords. This webinar explores how behavioral AI can help security teams detect compromised accounts faster and automate response workflows. [...

articles.title: From Assistive to Agentic: The AI Shift That's Redefining Threat Management
articles.summary: Introduction The average enterprise security team has 40 or more security tools, giving a lot of visibility into internal telemetry and asset data. articles.title: CryptoBandits Malware Doubles as a Backdoor, Abuses Tor
articles.summary: CryptoBandits uses a local SOCKS5 proxy for traffic routing, blending data theft with remote code execution. The post CryptoBandits Malware Doubles as a Backdoor, Abuses Tor appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: FortiBleed: 86,000 Fortinet Device Credentials Compromised
articles.summary: The large-scale credential theft campaign hit roughly half of the internet-accessible Fortinet firewalls and VPNs. The post FortiBleed: 86,000 Fortinet Device Credentials Compromised appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: CISA: Splunk Enterprise flaw actively exploited, patch by Sunday
articles.summary: CISA has urged U.S. federal agencies to secure their systems by Sunday against a critical Splunk Enterprise vulnerability that is being exploited in attacks. [...]

articles.title: Forget Data Leakage: Shadow AI's Real Threat Is Access Control
articles.summary: The first wave of enterprise AI concern was straightforward. It was simply employees pasting sensitive data into public AI tools. Security teams responded with usage policies, domain blocks, and data loss prevention rules. That response made sense at the time. It doesn't fit the problem anymore. Sha

articles.title: Cybersecurity Firms Impacted by Klue Supply Chain Attack
articles.summary: The hackers exfiltrated data from Salesforce instances of Klue customers, such as Huntress and Recorded Future. The post Cybersecurity Firms Impacted by Klue Supply Chain Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Cisco to Acquire WideField Security to Boost Splunk’s Agentic SOC
articles.summary: WideField will accelerate Agentic SOC capabilities by expanding the lens on threat investigation to include identity, credentials, sessions, and blast radius. The post Cisco to Acquire WideField Security to Boost Splunk’s Agentic SOC appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: CISA warns Fortinet users to secure devices after FortiBleed leak
articles.summary: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) urged Fortinet customers to secure their devices after nearly 74,000 firewall and VPN credentials were exposed in a data leak dubbed "FortiBleed." [...]

articles.title: Apple Patches Beats Studio Buds Flaw Letting Nearby Attackers Spy via Microphone
articles.summary: Apple has updated its Beats Studio Buds wireless earbuds to patch a high-severity vulnerability that could be exploited by nearby hackers to eavesdrop on users. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2025-20701 (CVSS score: 8.8), refers to a case of incorrect authorization impacting the Airoha Bluetooth

articles.title: Splunk Enterprise Vulnerability Exploited in Attacks Days After Disclosure
articles.summary: CISA has given federal agencies only three days to patch CVE-2026-20253, which can be exploited for unauthenticated remote code execution. The post Splunk Enterprise Vulnerability Exploited in Attacks Days After Disclosure appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Gentlemen ransomware uses multiple EDR killers to disable defenses
articles.summary: The Gentlemen ransomware-as-a-service (RaaS) is actively developing and maintaining a suite of endpoint detection and response (EDR) killers to help affiliates evade detection in attacks. [...]

articles.title: Nintendo confirms data stolen in WebMD subsidiary cyberattack
articles.summary: Nintendo of America has confirmed to BleepingComputer that threat actors stole survey data from the third-party TinyPulse service used internally, but its systems were not compromised. [...]

articles.title: ‘Popa’ Botnet Linked to Publicly-Traded Israeli Firm
articles.summary: For the past four years, a sprawling Android-based botnet called Popa has forced millions of consumer TV boxes to relay Internet traffic linked to advertising fraud, account takeovers, and mass data-scraping efforts. This week, researchers from multiple security firms concluded that the Popa botnet

articles.title: F5 Patches Two Critical NGINX Open Source Flaws Enabling Remote Code Execution
articles.summary: F5 has released security updates to address two critical security flaws in NGINX Open Source that could be exploited to achieve code execution on affected systems. articles.title: USB worm spreads crypto-stealing malware via Windows shortcut files
articles.summary: Threat actors targeting cryptocurrency wallets have been distributing clipboard-stealing malware with self-spreading capabilities and using the Tor network to conceal communication. [...]

articles.title: Microsoft Details Windows Clipper Malware Campaign Using USB LNK Worm and Tor-Based C2
articles.summary: Microsoft has disclosed details of a Windows-based cryptocurrency clipper campaign that has targeted users since February 2026 with clipboard-intercepting malware with self-spreading capabilities and using the Tor anonymity network to hide communication. articles.title: Australian sugar producer works to restore operations as ransomware group claims attack
articles.summary: Mackay Sugar said it was "working urgently" to verify claims that a highly active ransomware group was behind a cyberattack that shut down harvesting and milling operations.

articles.title: Klue OAuth breach linked to 'Icarus' Salesforce data theft attacks
articles.summary: Market intelligence platform Klue suffered a OAuth breach that enabled the "Icarus" threat actors to steal Salesforce CRM data from multiple organizations in an ongoing extortion campaign. [...]

articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have charted the evolution of INC from an nascent ransomware-as-a-service (RaaS) operation to one of the most prolific cybercrime groups in 2026, claiming no less than 830 victims since August 2023. articles.title: DragonForce Hackers Abuse Microsoft Teams Relays to Hide Backdoor.Turn C2 Traffic
articles.summary: Threat actors associated with the DragonForce ransomware have been observed using a custom Go-based remote access trojan (RAT) called Backdoor.Turn to conceal command-and-control (C2) traffic inside Microsoft Teams relay infrastructure. According to findings from Broadcom-owned Symantec and Carbon B

articles.title: Police cleans nearly 15,000 SocGholish-infected sites tied to Evil Corp
articles.summary: International law enforcement agencies cleaned nearly 15,000 malware-infected WordPress websites and took down more than 100 servers linked to the SocGholish botnet and the Evil Corp Russian cybercrime group. [...]

articles.title: ShapedPlugin update flow hacked to infect WordPress sites
articles.summary: Multiple WordPress plugins from ShapedPlugin were compromised in a supply chain attack that distributed infected releases to paying customers via the vendor's official update system. [...]

articles.title: FortiBleed leak exposes Fortinet VPN credentials for 73,000 devices.
articles.summary: A newly discovered data leak dubbed "FortiBleed" has exposed what appears to be a collection of Fortinet and FortiGate VPN credentials for 73,932 firewall URLs at organizations worldwide. [...]

articles.title: No Exploits Required
articles.summary: Four decades of incident response experience suggest that exploits are often the symptom, not the root cause, of today’s cybersecurity failures. The post No Exploits Required appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Apple fixes Beats Studio Buds flaw that let hackers spy on conversations
articles.summary: Apple has released security updates to patch a high-severity flaw affecting the Beats Studio Buds wireless earbuds that could allow attackers in Bluetooth range to spy on users' conversations. [...]

articles.title: F5 issues out-of-band patches for critical NGINX vulnerabilities
articles.summary: Cybersecurity company F5 has released out-of-band security updates to address multiple NGINX web server vulnerabilities, including two critical-severity flaws that could allow attackers to execute code on vulnerable systems. [...]

articles.title: Atlassian, Splunk Patch Critical Vulnerabilities
articles.summary: Splunk patched an OS command injection in AI Toolkit, while Atlassian fixed dozens of flaws in third-party dependencies. The post Atlassian, Splunk Patch Critical Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Rokarolla Banking Trojan Targets 200 Applications
articles.summary: The Android malware allows its operators to take control of infected devices and harvest sensitive information. The post Rokarolla Banking Trojan Targets 200 Applications appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Critical Command Execution Vulnerability Patched in Cisco ISE
articles.summary: Insufficient validation of user input allows an attacker to gain access to the underlying OS and elevate their privileges to root. The post Critical Command Execution Vulnerability Patched in Cisco ISE appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: F5 Patches Critical, High-Severity NGINX Vulnerabilities
articles.summary: Critical flaws in NGINX could allow remote, unauthenticated attackers to cause a restart and potentially execute arbitrary code. The post F5 Patches Critical, High-Severity NGINX Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Kodak Admits Data Breach After ShinyHunters Hack Claims
articles.summary: Kodak told SecurityWeek it believes there is no threat to its systems or operations as a result of the cybersecurity incident. The post Kodak Admits Data Breach After ShinyHunters Hack Claims appeared first on SecurityWeek.
